intent: In DATA_DIR there is a 10x matrix triplet for one PBMC sample — files prefixed GSM5746268_MGI0369_1_SLAB-C1-C1 (matrix.mtx.gz, barcodes.tsv.gz, features.tsv.gz). Using scanpy, do standard QC, normalization, highly-variable genes, PCA, neighbors, Leiden clustering and a UMAP, then identify marker genes per cluster and show me a UMAP and a marker dotplot.

blocks.text: Good — the data is there. Now let me read the `scrna-qc-clustering` skill to understand the best approach:

blocks.content: {"status": "ok", "name": "scrna-qc-clustering", "description": "Standard scanpy processing for ONE scRNA-seq sample.", "body": "scRNA-seq QC + first-pass clustering (scanpy). Halt and present_plan before running on an unfamiliar dataset \u2014 the QC thresholds and the clustering resolution are dataset-dependent. Steps: sc.read_10x_mtx; calculate_qc_metrics; filter cells (n_genes>=200) & genes; filter pct_counts_mt; normalize_total(target_sum=1e4)+log1p; highly_variable_genes; scale; pca; neighbors; leiden; umap; rank_genes_groups (wilcoxon) for markers. Plots: QC violin, HVG, UMAP colored by leiden, marker dotplot."}

blocks.text: Perfect — this matches exactly what you're asking for. Let me now present the plan, pausing before execution so you can review and adjust any QC thresholds or clustering parameters if needed:

input.title: Standard scRNA-seq QC & clustering on PBMC sample GSM5746268
input.summary: Load 10x triplet, QC-filter, normalize, identify highly-variable genes, PCA, clustering, UMAP, and marker detection.
input.rationale: Provides a data-driven first pass on a single PBMC sample: visualize cell populations and identify marker genes per cluster.
input.assumptions:
- QC filtering will use standard thresholds: n_genes >= 200, mitochondrial genes filtered at a moderate threshold (typically pct_counts_mt < 20%), and genes in at least 3 cells.
  - Leiden clustering resolution will be set to a default (0.5–1.0) suitable for PBMCs; can be adjusted post-hoc if clusters are too granular or coarse.
  - Marker genes identified via Wilcoxon rank-sum test (standard in scanpy).
  - Species is human (standard for PBMC); gene names use HGNC symbols from the features file.

steps.title: Load 10x matrix triplet into AnnData
steps.description: Read the gzipped MTX, barcodes, and features files using sc.read_10x_mtx.
expected_outputs: AnnData object with ~16k genes × ~8–10k cells, sparse matrix

steps.title: Calculate QC metrics
steps.description: sc.pp.calculate_qc_metrics: n_genes, n_counts, pct_counts_mt (identify mitochondrial genes by 'MT-' prefix).
expected_outputs: QC metadata in .obs and .var; QC violin plots

steps.title: Filter cells and genes
steps.description: Remove cells with <200 genes, genes in <3 cells. Filter pct_counts_mt at a reasonable cutoff (e.g., <20%).

steps.title: Identify highly-variable genes (HVG)
expected_outputs: HVG boolean in .var; HVG scatter plot

steps.title: Build neighborhood graph and cluster
expected_outputs: Neighborhood indices in .obsp; Leiden cluster labels in .obs

steps.title: Identify marker genes per cluster
expected_outputs: Ranked genes in .uns['rank_genes_groups']; top markers per cluster

steps.title: Visualize: UMAP and marker dotplot
steps.description: sc.pl.umap colored by leiden; sc.pl.dotplot for top markers across clusters.
